title: Speaker of the Riksdag

([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Riksdagens talman*) is the [presiding officer](/wiki/Speaker_(politics) "Speaker (politics)") of the national [unicameral](/wiki/Unicameral "Unicameral") [legislature](/wiki/Legislature "Legislature") in [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Presiding officer of Swedish legislature

The **speaker of the [Riksdag](/wiki/Riksdag "Riksdag")** ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Riksdagens talman*) is the [presiding officer](/wiki/Speaker_(politics) "Speaker (politics)") of the national [unicameral](/wiki/Unicameral "Unicameral") [legislature](/wiki/Legislature "Legislature") in [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden").

The Riksdag underwent profound changes in 1867, when the medieval [Riksdag of the Estates](/wiki/Riksdag_of_the_Estates "Riksdag of the Estates") was abolished. The new form of the Riksdag included [two elected chambers](/wiki/Bicameral_system "Bicameral system"), each with its own speaker. Since the *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* introduction of [parliamentarism](/wiki/Parliamentarism "Parliamentarism") in 1917, the Riksdag has properly functioned as the institution to which the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_minister "Prime minister") and the [government](/wiki/Government_of_Sweden "Government of Sweden") are held accountable. In 1971 the institution was transformed into a unicameral legislature with 350 members, reduced to 349 in 1976 to avoid parliamentary deadlocks. Since 1975, in accordance with the [Instrument of Government of 1974](/wiki/Basic_Laws_of_Sweden#Instrument_of_Government "Basic Laws of Sweden"), it is the speaker and no longer the [monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Sweden "Monarchy of Sweden") who appoints and dismisses the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Sweden "Prime Minister of Sweden").

The current speaker is [Andreas Norlén](/wiki/Andreas_Norl%C3%A9n "Andreas Norlén"), who has held the [gavel](/wiki/Gavel "Gavel") since September 2018.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

| * flag [Sweden portal](/wiki/Portal:Sweden "Portal:Sweden")   * [Other countries](/wiki/Outline_of_political_science#Politics_by_region "Outline of political science") |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Politics_of_Sweden "Template:Politics of Sweden") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Politics_of_Sweden "Template talk:Politics of Sweden") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Politics_of_Sweden "Special:EditPage/Template:Politics of Sweden") |

The speaker is the head and presiding officer of the [Riksdag](/wiki/Riksdag_of_Sweden "Riksdag of Sweden"), and is elected by the chamber as the first order of business when the Riksdag re-convenes following a [general election](/wiki/Elections_in_Sweden "Elections in Sweden"). As such, the speaker coordinates the work that takes place in the Riksdag. The office is mandated in the [Swedish constitution](/wiki/Swedish_constitution "Swedish constitution") and the duties of the office are set out in the [Instrument of Government (1974)](/wiki/1974_Instrument_of_Government "1974 Instrument of Government") and the Riksdag Act.

The speaker themselves does not take part in debates, nor do they participate in the parliamentary committees. Consequently, they have no vote, and a substitute is appointed to fill his or her seat in the Riksdag during their time in office. While the speaker is still one of the elected representatives of the Riksdag, they are expected to remain unbiased and objective with regards to the political issues that are debated.

In terms of official protocol, the position of speaker is the second highest-ranked public position in Sweden. Only the [monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Sweden "Monarchy of Sweden") outranks the speaker since the monarch is the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state"). Additionally, the speaker nominally outranks the [prime minister of Sweden](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Sweden "Prime Minister of Sweden"), even though (since the 1974–75 reforms) that person is the country's *de jure* and *de facto* chief executive.[[3]](#cite_note-RdSpeaker-3)

### Appointment and dismissal of the prime minister

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Speaker_of_the_Riksdag&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Appointment and dismissal of the prime minister")]

One of the more important aspects of the work of the speaker is to head negotiations concerning the forming of a new government in case there is a shift of power after an election. The speaker can then dismiss a prime minister who is voted out of office, which happened for the first time on 25 September 2018.[[4]](#cite_note-4) After the negotiations, the speaker proposes the new prime ministerial candidate to the chamber, and following a positive vote, the speaker signs the [commission](/wiki/Commission_(document) "Commission (document)") ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *förordnande*) on behalf of the Riksdag. The prime minister appoints and dismisses their own cabinet ministers, forming the [government](/wiki/Government_of_Sweden "Government of Sweden") ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Regeringen*), without the involvement of the speaker.[[3]](#cite_note-RdSpeaker-3)[[5]](#cite_note-RdFormGov-5)

In case of either a voluntary resignation or a [vote of no confidence](/wiki/Vote_of_no_confidence "Vote of no confidence"), the letter of resignation of a prime minister is handed to the speaker.

In most other [parliamentary systems](/wiki/Parliamentarism "Parliamentarism"), including other [constitutional monarchies](/wiki/Constitutional_monarchy "Constitutional monarchy"), these duties are instead handled by the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state"). Relieving the Swedish monarch of political powers, although not the key objective from the outset, nevertheless became an important part of constitutional reform in the 1970s.[*[according to whom?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Words_to_watch#Unsupported_attributions "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Words to watch")*]

The speaker also attends the special change-of-government council held at the [Stockholm Palace](/wiki/Stockholm_Palace "Stockholm Palace") following the appointment of a new prime minister. During these meetings the speaker informs the [monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Sweden "Monarchy of Sweden") on the procedure of forming the incoming government. The monarch then announces the change of government.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Deputy speakers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Speaker_of_the_Riksdag&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Deputy speakers")]

Main article: [List of deputy speakers of the Riksdag](/wiki/List_of_deputy_speakers_of_the_Riksdag "List of deputy speakers of the Riksdag")

The speaker is assisted by three deputy speakers who are also elected by the chamber.[[7]](#cite_note-RdDep-7) Traditionally, the second, third and fourth largest parties gets to name of one of their members for these offices. There is some disagreement whether the *largest party* or the *leading party of the largest party bloc* should hold the speakership (and thus also the position of [First Deputy Speaker](/wiki/First_Deputy_Speaker_of_the_Riksdag "First Deputy Speaker of the Riksdag")).
Unlike the Speaker (and cabinet ministers), the deputy Speakers are not replaced by an alternate and remain members of the Riksdag with voting rights.[[7]](#cite_note-RdDep-7)

### Regent ad interim

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Speaker_of_the_Riksdag&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Regent ad interim")]

In case all adult members of the [Swedish royal family](/wiki/Swedish_royal_family "Swedish royal family") who are in the [line of succession to the throne](/wiki/Line_of_succession_to_the_Swedish_throne "Line of succession to the Swedish throne"), as prescribed in the [Act of Succession](/wiki/Swedish_Act_of_Succession "Swedish Act of Succession"), are out of the country, the speaker assumes the role of *Regent ad interim* ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Riksföreståndare*). This would also be the case if they were all to decease.

Below are listed all speakers who have acted as Regent ad interim:

* [Ingemund Bengtsson](/wiki/Ingemund_Bengtsson "Ingemund Bengtsson") (2-3 July 1988)

### Riksdag Board

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Speaker_of_the_Riksdag&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Riksdag Board")]

The speaker chairs the Riksdag Board ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Riksdagsstyrelsen*), which deliberates on the organisation of the work of the Riksdag, directs the work of the Riksdag Administration ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Riksdagsförvaltningen*) and decides upon matters of major significance concerning the international contacts programme.

### War Delegation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Speaker_of_the_Riksdag&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: War Delegation")]

The speaker chairs the [War Delegation](/wiki/War_Delegation "War Delegation") ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *krigsdelegationen*), when it is deemed necessary to convene.
